nodes.rationale: Read-only tracking + the two D9 gates in the browser; drive capability later; council gate approvable from a phone.
nodes.norm_label: d21 — gui mvp: observe + approve gates first

nodes.label: D22 — Hosting: claude.narenwebworks.in EC2 (t4g.large)
nodes.rationale: Initial hosting, revisit on load/work adoption.

nodes.label: D23 — Setup wizard: full scope
nodes.rationale: init + graphify/spec-kit + dev server + agent library bootstrap + MCP registration + autonomy profiles; ships last, packages everything.

nodes.label: D24 — Self-evolving year-one boundary: agent library only (flywheel)
nodes.rationale: Self-tuning config and self-regenerating wizard out of scope until the flywheel proves the pattern.
nodes.norm_label: d24 — self-evolving year-one boundary: agent library only (flywheel)

nodes.label: D25 — Git ext v1: branch-before-plan + per-feature lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Branch naming from spec ID, phase commit conventions, cleanup; worktrees-per-wave scoped as a timeboxed spike, not committed scope.
nodes.norm_label: d25 — git ext v1: branch-before-plan + per-feature lifecycle

nodes.label: D26 — System lives in a brand-new repo (specseyal, monorepo)
nodes.rationale: extensions/ + platform/ + docs/; graphify migrates into extensions/graphify/; speckit-graphifyy archived at checkpoint alpha.
nodes.norm_label: d26 — system lives in a brand-new repo (specseyal, monorepo)

nodes.label: D27 — License: MIT
nodes.rationale: Confirmed.

nodes.label: D28 — Billing: subscription-only through M7
nodes.rationale: M0-M5 interactive Claude Code + no-AI manager; M6+ on the Agent SDK monthly credit; keep ANTHROPIC_API_KEY unset so billing never silently routes to the API.

nodes.label: D29 — New repo private until checkpoint alpha
nodes.rationale: Public when the full CLI pipeline demonstrably runs (end of M4); best-first-impression launch.
nodes.norm_label: d29 — new repo private until checkpoint alpha

nodes.label: D30 — Name: SpecSeyal
nodes.rationale: seyal = action (Tamil); spec -> action; GitHub and npm namespaces verified clean.

nodes.label: D31 — Graphify migration by git subtree add (no squash)
nodes.rationale: Upstream had one commit so history preservation was free and git-filter-repo unnecessary; behavior unmodified (M0 constraint).
nodes.norm_label: d31 — graphify migration by git subtree add (no squash)

nodes.label: D32 — Phase state inferred from artifacts; no state file, ever
nodes.rationale: Principle 3 made mechanical; gate decisions are artifacts not events; branch is the sole phase whose state lives outside specs/ (the git ref).
nodes.norm_label: d32 — phase state inferred from artifacts; no state file, ever

nodes.label: D33 — Absent profile.yaml => both gates human; full_auto handshake
nodes.rationale: full_auto is redundant as data but load-bearing as ceremony — it makes the diff that disables adversarial review impossible to read as an accident.
nodes.norm_label: d33 — absent profile.yaml => both gates human; full_auto handshake

nodes.label: D34 — Library entries are Claude Code agent files; join key is id, not name
nodes.rationale: The file must dispatch as a subagent today and sync to the central library tomorrow with no conversion; joining on name would orphan a specialist's stats on first rename.
nodes.norm_label: d34 — library entries are claude code agent files; join key is id, not name

nodes.label: D35 — Traces: append-only JSONL, no message bodies, cost_usd null
nodes.rationale: No-bodies serves context hygiene and makes traces safe to sync multi-tenant without redaction; tokens are the unit of account; a synthesized cost_usd would be summed and eventually believed.
nodes.norm_label: d35 — traces: append-only jsonl, no message bodies, cost_usd null

nodes.label: D36 — Taxonomy v0 DRAFT (9 types x 10 spec) — superseded by D42
nodes.rationale: No integration type (its work goes to the orchestrator); type is graph-derivable, specialization is not — the asymmetry justifying two axes and a Sonnet categorizer.
nodes.norm_label: d36 — taxonomy v0 draft (9 types x 10 spec) — superseded by d42

nodes.label: D37 — Categorize writes categorization.md, never mutates tasks.md
nodes.rationale: One artifact out keeps phases resumable; tasks.md already has two writers, a third would make it the pipeline's shared-mutable file — the hazard graphify exists to detect.
nodes.norm_label: d37 — categorize writes categorization.md, never mutates tasks.md

nodes.label: D38 — Council deck is not round-scoped; overwritten in place
nodes.rationale: Git on the feature branch is the deck's version store; copying it per round duplicates what git already holds.
nodes.norm_label: d38 — council deck is not round-scoped; overwritten in place

nodes.label: D39 — Two contracts/ directories documented, not renamed
nodes.rationale: The collision is inherited from spec-kit; renaming either breaks a convention someone else owns. Always qualify the path.
nodes.norm_label: d39 — two contracts/ directories documented, not renamed

nodes.label: D40 — Composability (A-1): the unit of storage is the SKILL, not the agent
nodes.rationale: An agent is an assembly at dispatch; tags gain a third job (skill selection); the gap generator becomes a skill builder; assembly cap of 3, additive-only; grows composable parts not near-empty lanes.
nodes.norm_label: d40 — composability (a-1): the unit of storage is the skill, not the agent

nodes.label: D41 — Tool grants (A-2): per-skill declared, incl. web search
nodes.rationale: The assembled agent's grant set is the union of its skills' declarations; the workforce-gate roster MUST display it — approving the roster is approving network access.
nodes.norm_label: d41 — tool grants (a-2): per-skill declared, incl. web search

nodes.label: D42 — Taxonomy v0 BLESSED (6 OQ verdicts, 8x11, general cap 20%)
nodes.rationale: OQ1 overruled (refactor is a modifier, not a type); one specialization; general capped, over-cap FAILS; scaffold/infra stay separate because composability removed the cost of near-empty lanes.
nodes.norm_label: d42 — taxonomy v0 blessed (6 oq verdicts, 8x11, general cap 20%)

nodes.label: D43 — Trace record carries the assembly (skills[], elevated_grants)
nodes.rationale: Unblocks the flywheel D40 stranded — the pre-amendment record named no injected skills so skill-level attribution was uncomputable; stats rekeyed on skill id@version so a regressed version cannot coast on an older one.
nodes.norm_label: d43 — trace record carries the assembly (skills[], elevated_grants)

nodes.label: D44 — Flag adjudications (cap stands, two-kind storage, core vs elevated terms)
nodes.rationale: Bases are curated-static, only skills evolve; core toolset vs elevated grant codified as defined terms; A-2 governs elevated access only.
nodes.norm_label: d44 — flag adjudications (cap stands, two-kind storage, core vs elevated terms)

nodes.label: D45 — M1 tooling bootstrap: real spec-kit + graphify installed for dogfooding
nodes.rationale: Makes the dogfooding rule concrete; the working graph is gitignored transient state, durable phase state lives only under specs/NNN.
nodes.norm_label: d45 — m1 tooling bootstrap: real spec-kit + graphify installed for dogfooding

nodes.label: D46 — M1 spec-review adjudications (001)
nodes.rationale: FR-017 split (manual reopen interface v1); no build-phase trace backfill; standing spec-hygiene rule (every Constraints entry cites a D-row); reduced-grounding must be visible at the gate.

nodes.label: D47 — Trace token capture is exact-or-null, with a recorded method
nodes.rationale: capture_method in {sdk, transcript, unavailable}; never a fabricated estimate (same ethos as cost_usd null); spend rollups skip null-token records and report their count.
nodes.norm_label: d47 — trace token capture is exact-or-null, with a recorded method

nodes.label: D48 — M3 guard: prompt-tagged tasks keep the D18 Sonnet floor
nodes.rationale: A prompt/deck template is mechanically docs but prompt authoring is ai-agents implementation work; without a guard it could escape the Sonnet floor via a docs-exempt base. Re-homed onto runtime_consumed at v1.
nodes.norm_label: d48 — m3 guard: prompt-tagged tasks keep the d18 sonnet floor

nodes.label: D49 — Workforce-gate record format defined (## Workforce Gate, artifact-layout §8)
nodes.rationale: A gate with no record format is a contracts bug; homed in assignment.md (not council-owned decision-record.md) with a mandatory Elevated grants column.
nodes.norm_label: d49 — workforce-gate record format defined (## workforce gate, artifact-layout §8)

nodes.label: D50 — Conformance rule-5 meta-feature carve-out
nodes.rationale: A council-building spec must describe opinions/ to specify it; exempt iff the spec carries a self-declared marker — mechanical (still a grep), reasoned, and local.

nodes.label: D51 — M2 spec-review adjudications (002)
nodes.rationale: Branch born at specify (base stays clean); --no-ff unconditional (the merge commit is the D19/M5 completion anchor); stale-approval re-approval routes by gate type.

nodes.label: D52 — Completion anchor becomes a complete/<spec-id> tag; ff-permitted
nodes.rationale: An annotated tag serves the binding + enumeration purpose independent of merge topology, so merge policy relaxes to ff-permitted; supersedes D51's merge clause only.
nodes.norm_label: d52 — completion anchor becomes a complete/<spec-id> tag; ff-permitted

nodes.label: D53 — Hook enforcement is prose-level in v1; mechanical HookExecutor deferred to M6
nodes.rationale: The first live council found no code-level hook enforcement; v1 accepts this honestly; the M6 Agent SDK orchestrator gives programmatic enforcement. A decision, not an omission.
nodes.norm_label: d53 — hook enforcement is prose-level in v1; mechanical hookexecutor deferred to m6

nodes.label: D54 — Wave-worktree spike hard-capped <=2h; a null result is a success
nodes.rationale: An unbounded timebox is untestable; a spike concluding worktree-per-wave does not pay is a knowledge deliverable. Resolved at M2: ABANDON.
nodes.norm_label: d54 — wave-worktree spike hard-capped <=2h; a null result is a success

nodes.label: D55 — FR-008 gate-section = well-known-path convention; pointer ownership to the gate command
nodes.rationale: The git ext writes only gates.yml, never the council-owned artifact; the pointer line is the gate command's job on its next touch — keeps principle I clean (no second writer).
nodes.norm_label: d55 — fr-008 gate-section = well-known-path convention; pointer ownership to the gate command

nodes.label: D56 — Council ceremony tiers + lazy context grounding
nodes.rationale: Lands the M2 5.25M finding; full (12 sessions) default vs standard (8 sessions, consolidated peer + lazy context + output cap); council_tier is the first cost lever, member_count the second.
nodes.norm_label: d56 — council ceremony tiers + lazy context grounding

nodes.label: D57 — Cross-extension seam convention: hook points over source edits
nodes.rationale: Every install.sh rm -rf + cp -R wipes a foreign edit; an unavoidable edit lives in the owning extension's source and is reinstall-survival-tested; promotes I-14 to normative.
nodes.norm_label: d57 — cross-extension seam convention: hook points over source edits

nodes.label: D58 — M3 spec-review adjudications (003)
nodes.rationale: Pipeline order analyze-before-categorize (classification follows stabilization); determinism boundary made checkable from the artifact (library vs built marks, FR-022).

nodes.label: D59 — Tiered-council baseline graph snapshotted + committed
nodes.rationale: A scoped one-time exception to D45's never-committed working graph; a baseline measurement deserves exact reproducibility; the live graph stays gitignored so no diff-noise.
nodes.norm_label: d59 — tiered-council baseline graph snapshotted + committed

nodes.label: D60 — web_search GRANTED to the skill-builder — the first elevated grant
nodes.rationale: A builder authoring skills for post-cutoff frameworks without search produces confidently-stale modules; granting search + flagging non-search (stale_risk) is the honest resolution; audit chain stays intact.
nodes.norm_label: d60 — web_search granted to the skill-builder — the first elevated grant

nodes.label: D61 — council_tier default flips to standard (D56 amended)
nodes.rationale: First live standard council cost -46.1% and rigor held (2 real blockers); the first tuning decision the system made on its own data (D6 cashing out); N=1 revisit clause.
nodes.norm_label: d61 — council_tier default flips to standard (d56 amended)

nodes.label: D62 — Deck-prep improvement: mine council read-rate to enrich the deck
nodes.rationale: 4/5 members opened plan.md because the deck under-served them; mine which sections they pulled and ride them in the technical deck; after-metric is the next run's read-rate.
nodes.norm_label: d62 — deck-prep improvement: mine council read-rate to enrich the deck

nodes.label: D63 — Capability authorization != dispatch approval
nodes.rationale: An A-2 grant derives only from a skill declaration injected into an assembly; a gate approves dispatches with the grants their work needs; network reach on a roster = union of injected-skill grants, never a curated display.

nodes.label: D64 — Meta-feature gate-machinery: grandfather the checks it cannot apply to itself; fix its own bugs
nodes.rationale: A feature that builds the workforce gate-write cannot be governed by it; grandfather the checks (Option A) but never ship the guard broken; never hand-wave past a hard-block or hand-edit gates.yml.
nodes.norm_label: d64 — meta-feature gate-machinery: grandfather the checks it cannot apply to itself; fix its own bugs

nodes.label: D65 — Taxonomy v0 -> v1 BLESSED (verdicts 1-10)
nodes.rationale: Three-feature dogfood review; §1 derivability honesty + path-convention fallback; runtime_consumed modifier; floor'd general cap; agt_devtools_cli accepts docs (2.0.0); OQ6/OQ4 untestable on dogfood, deferred to v2.

nodes.label: D66 — Flywheel economics: gap-batching primary + evidence-backed seeding only
nodes.rationale: The builder clusters gaps by tag similarity and authors one skill per cluster; speculative mass-seeding rejected (a skill with no task is a guess), pure 1:1 firing rejected (fan-out on every sparse feature); assemble.py computes GAP_CLUSTERS deterministically.
nodes.norm_label: d66 — flywheel economics: gap-batching primary + evidence-backed seeding only

nodes.label: D67 — Workforce auto-mode valid standalone, WITH a grant tripwire (resolves I-19)
nodes.rationale: P4 wins over FR-020's coupling (D8/D9 never coupled the gates; the workforce gate is an economic guard); any elevated grant in the roster forces the gate to a human regardless of profile — approving it is approving network access.
nodes.norm_label: d67 — workforce auto-mode valid standalone, with a grant tripwire (resolves i-19)

nodes.label: D68 — M4 spec-review adjudications (004)
nodes.rationale: Doc-only testing boundary; completion report as a validatable D19 artifact.body; both gates human to exercise the first machine-bound workforce gate; I-17 ruled in-scope as a distinct Prerequisite FR-group so M4's own implement runs waves 2+ under the fixed machinery.

nodes.label: I-1 — Library flywheel
nodes.rationale: Generated agents/skills that perform get persisted into the library with usage stats.

nodes.label: I-2 — Graph-driven complexity scoring for model assignment
nodes.rationale: Fan-in/out and file count as proxies; needs Q6/observability data.
nodes.norm_label: i-2 — graph-driven complexity scoring for model assignment

nodes.label: I-3 — Testing agent v2 (actually run tests)
nodes.rationale: Run tests not just produce the doc; feed failures back as remediation tasks; post-v1.
nodes.norm_label: i-3 — testing agent v2 (actually run tests)

nodes.label: I-4 — Git worktrees per implementation wave (RESOLVED: abandon)
nodes.rationale: Adds cost for zero benefit on disjoint-file waves and is worse on shared files; the DAG is the correct isolation layer. Null result = success (D54).
nodes.norm_label: i-4 — git worktrees per implementation wave (resolved: abandon)

nodes.label: I-5 — Council reviews implementation too
nodes.rationale: A post-implement council round on the completion report; parked (council spec non-goal v1).

nodes.label: I-6 — Non-technical deck doubles as stakeholder artifact
nodes.rationale: Reusable in work contexts; free byproduct of the technical/non-technical deck split.
nodes.norm_label: i-6 — non-technical deck doubles as stakeholder artifact

nodes.label: I-7 — Open-source the orchestrator
nodes.rationale: Agent SDK-native Spec Kit orchestration appears an unoccupied niche; promoted by D7 (OSS simultaneous with work adoption).

nodes.label: I-8 — Suggestion classification taxonomy generalizes to analyze/remediation
nodes.rationale: blocking/strong/consider could feed D11's severity routing; cheap consistency win.
nodes.norm_label: i-8 — suggestion classification taxonomy generalizes to analyze/remediation

nodes.label: I-9 — Domain critics for work contexts
nodes.rationale: e.g. a compliance/HIPAA critic auto-added to the council v2 roster per repo/domain; fits v2's prompt-config-only evolution.
nodes.norm_label: i-9 — domain critics for work contexts

nodes.label: I-10 — Central skill-registry MCP
nodes.rationale: An MCP server exposing the shared skill bench; reframed by D40 from agent bench to skill registry since the skill is the unit that syncs; D17 preps for it via stable IDs.

nodes.label: I-11 — Conformance checker for docs/contracts/
nodes.rationale: A script validating any specs/NNN against the contract schemas in CI; must be rewritten from docs/contracts alone (the fixture-written one certifies its own misreadings). Carve-out resolved D50; the build remains open.

nodes.label: I-12 — Workforce-gate record format undefined (resolved D49)
nodes.rationale: A gate with no record format is a contracts bug; 001 had to record its gate as a flagged addendum in decision-record.md.
nodes.norm_label: i-12 — workforce-gate record format undefined (resolved d49)

nodes.label: I-13 — graphify coverage for non-code artifacts (.sh/.yml/.md)
nodes.rationale: graphify returns no node for shell/YAML, so plumbing features can't graph-ground blast-radius claims; the taxonomy §1 path-convention fallback is the standing basis until this lands; graphify backlog, not a taxonomy patch.
nodes.norm_label: i-13 — graphify coverage for non-code artifacts (.sh/.yml/.md)

nodes.label: I-14 — Cross-extension seam convention (hook points over source edits)
nodes.rationale: The reinstall-survival regression caught it live at implement wave 12; promoted to normative by D57.
nodes.norm_label: i-14 — cross-extension seam convention (hook points over source edits)

nodes.label: I-15 — docs x devtools-cli lane demand (resolved D65)
nodes.rationale: 003's README task matched no lane and fell to agt_generic; the fallback + empty-lane reporting behaved as designed — a demand signal, not a bug. Resolved by widening agt_devtools_cli to accept docs.
nodes.norm_label: i-15 — docs x devtools-cli lane demand (resolved d65)

nodes.label: I-16 — No bootstrap/grandfather workforce-binding path
nodes.rationale: verify-gate workforce fail-closes on the meta-feature that builds the binding hook; git-ext needs a documented grandfather escape. Grandfathered N/A for 003 (Option A).

nodes.label: I-17 — [X]-marking during implement stales the workforce binding (general defect)
nodes.rationale: Marking [X] per wave makes tasks.md stale so the per-wave re-verify hard-blocks wave 2+; git-ext must re-bind per wave or exclude check-state; ruled in-scope for M4 by D68.
nodes.norm_label: i-17 — [x]-marking during implement stales the workforce binding (general defect)

nodes.label: I-18 — git-ext workforce gate-write pointed at the wrong path (FIXED)
nodes.rationale: gates.sh defaulted to flat specs/<id>/assignment.md but the roster lives at agents/assignment.md; the first real workforce-approve would die. Fixed in the S02 cluster because 003 builds the gate-write and shipping it broken defeats the blocking finding.
nodes.norm_label: i-18 — git-ext workforce gate-write pointed at the wrong path (fixed)

nodes.label: I-19 — Contract inconsistency: workforce auto-mode scope (resolved D67)
nodes.rationale: FR-020 + commands.md gate workforce auto behind full_auto, but profile-schema P4 says it is valid standalone; the shipped T017 skill followed P4. Resolved in P4's favour with a grant tripwire.
nodes.norm_label: i-19 — contract inconsistency: workforce auto-mode scope (resolved d67)

nodes.label: Installed Extension Hook Registry (.specify/extensions.yml)
nodes.rationale: Deployed expansion of each extension's declared hooks into dispatch rows; installed=[graphify,git,workforce]; enforcement is prose-level in v1 (no HookExecutor) — the invoking phase-skill runs each hook and honors its exit code.

nodes.label: after_council_approve hook (installed)
nodes.rationale: Signer-agnostic (FR-010): fires even on an auto-written gate; dispatches speckit.git.record-gate to bind plan.md @ sha into gates.yml.

nodes.label: after_workforce_approve hook (installed)
nodes.rationale: The S02 seam: emitted by /speckit-workforce-approve but handled entirely by git-ext's record-gate; binds tasks.md + assignment.md @ sha into gates.yml (FR-010/W4, signer-agnostic).

nodes.label: before_tasks gate hook (installed)
nodes.rationale: git verify-gate (gate: council) inserted AHEAD of graphify by lower priority number (R1-S07); non-zero hard-blocks the tasks phase (FR-009).

nodes.label: before_implement gate hook (installed)
nodes.rationale: git verify-gate (gate: workforce) ahead of graphify; non-zero hard-blocks the implement phase (FR-009); re-run each wave by implement-parallel (R1-S23).

nodes.label: Council Extension Config (installed council-config.yml)
nodes.rationale: member_count=5, max_rounds=1, D18 model map (chairman opus, member/deck_prep sonnet); read by council, council-triage, council-approve.

nodes.label: Council full ceremony tier
nodes.rationale: M2 baseline (~5.25M billable tok/round): per_member peer review (member_count parallel sessions), eager context, no member_output_cap. Now opt-in via profile.yaml for L-sized / architecture-changing features.

nodes.label: Council standard ceremony tier (D56/D61 default)
nodes.rationale: Cost-controlled default flipped from full by D61 on the 003 measurement (-46.1% tok, 8 vs 12 sessions, rigor held): consolidated peer review (one session), lazy context (deck-only up front), member_output_cap 6.

nodes.label: Council member lenses (correctness/risk/simplicity/testability/sequencing)
nodes.rationale: Soft one-per-member analytical nudge (A-E) on the same base member-prompt (D18) — not the v2 role-critic roster; must have exactly member_count entries.

nodes.label: Consolidated peer-review stage (standard tier)
nodes.rationale: One neutral reviewer critiques all five stage-1 opinions at once (no lettered identity/lens), cap <=15 points, writes opinions/peer/consolidated.md — the standard-tier substitute for per_member peer review (D56).

nodes.label: Git Extension Manifest (installed extension.yml)
nodes.rationale: Per-Feature Git Lifecycle (D25): mechanical git only, zero AI, no traces.jsonl (FR-007). Installer expands each hook entry into a .specify/extensions.yml registry row; on before_tasks/before_implement inserts verify-gate ahead of graphify (R1-S07).

nodes.label: speckit.git.commit command
nodes.rationale: Phase-tagged commit in FR-006 grammar; self-heals the feature branch first (R1-S12); no-op on clean tree (FR-004); staging scoped to specs/NNN/** + declared outputs (R1-S11).

nodes.label: speckit.git.sha command
nodes.rationale: Read-only: prints the HEAD SHA that last touched an artifact — the value a gate records (FR-008).

nodes.label: speckit.git.verify-gate command
nodes.rationale: Compares gates.yml's recorded <artifact> @ <sha> against current; working-tree-aware + fail-closed (R1-S05/S10); exit non-zero on stale — the FR-009 hard-block.

nodes.label: speckit.git.cleanup command
nodes.rationale: Integrate into base (ff-permitted, D52) + mandatory annotated tag complete/<spec-id> + delete branch; conflict aborts and is surfaced (FR-011).

nodes.label: speckit.git.record-gate hook action
nodes.rationale: Hook-internal action (not a reusable command, absent from provides.commands): resolves to on-gate-approve.sh <council|workforce>, composing sha.sh + gates.sh to write the gate binding; signer-agnostic (FR-010).

nodes.label: on-gate-approve.sh (gate-agnostic gate-write script)
nodes.rationale: D57 S2 generalization of the previously council-only on-council-approve.sh into on-gate-approve.sh <council|workforce> (R8) — the one unavoidable source edit lives in git-ext because git owns gates.yml.

nodes.label: gates.yml (git-ext-owned gate binding store)
nodes.rationale: The artifact@sha binding record-gate writes and verify-gate checks; the freshness anchor for the council (plan.md) and workforce (tasks.md + assignment.md) hard-blocks.

nodes.label: Workforce Pair Extension (README, installed)
nodes.rationale: Packaged as one extension (council S10). Split: categorization=inference (Sonnet), assembly=code (zero-AI, byte-reproducible on gap-free run), skill-building=inference (Sonnet, only on a gap).

nodes.label: assemble.py (deterministic zero-AI matcher/assembler)
nodes.rationale: agent-library-schema.md §3 verbatim: base lookup by (type,specialization), tag-Jaccard-ranked skills, forced skl_refactor_discipline on preserves_behavior, cap 3 (D40); writes the roster table itself (S08); total_order() everywhere (S01) ⇒ SC-005 byte-identical. Exit 0 writes; 2=D48 guard; 3=malformed/invariant; 4=frontmatter parse fail.

nodes.label: validate-categorization.py (categorization code gate)
nodes.rationale: Sole authority on the categorize verdict (FR-005): re-derives 100% coverage + closed-enum (SC-001) + general cap as exact integer inequality general*5>total*1 (never a float). Exit 0=PASS write/keep; 1=FAIL nothing written (S22); 2=usage error.

nodes.label: validate-skill.py (generated-skill safety gate)
nodes.rationale: Frontmatter/id/version/origin shape, grants disjoint from core toolset (D41), additive-only proxies S1-S3, and S04 (generated taxonomy.tags MUST intersect the triggering task's tags — the check that actually closes the gap). Fail-closed; exit 0 only on all-pass.

nodes.label: frontmatter.py (shared specseyal: parser)
nodes.rationale: The one shared specseyal: parser (S21), imported by assemble.py and validate-skill.py; pure stdlib (R2 'no PyYAML'); understands only the closed shape and fails clearly (FrontmatterError). Also defines body_sha256().

nodes.label: The S02 seam (after_workforce_approve gate-write)
nodes.rationale: Round-1 council flagged S02 blocking: the workforce gate had no real gate-write path. Fix (D57 S2): git-ext generalized on-council-approve.sh into on-gate-approve.sh workforce; git's extension.yml — not workforce's — registers the handler, so registering a second handler here would double-fire the write.

nodes.label: Workforce command flow (categorize→agent-assign→approve→implement)
nodes.rationale: /speckit-analyze (D58) → categorize → agent-assign → workforce-approve → implement-parallel; each command reads what the previous wrote and writes exactly one artifact class. categorize runs after analyze because analyze can still patch tasks.md.

nodes.label: Workforce Extension Manifest (installed extension.yml)
nodes.rationale: id workforce; provides the 3 commands; after_categorize/after_agent-assign hooks target git-ext's OWN speckit.git.commit (no edit to git source, R7/D57 S1); after_workforce_approve deliberately NOT registered here (S25) — handled in git's manifest.

nodes.label: Roster approved table
nodes.rationale: One row per assembled agent (base + <=3 skills, each library|built FR-022); Elevated grants mandatory (none when empty, W2); empty-lane annotation on agt_generic fallback (FR-016); dropped-skill notes on cap trim (FR-011). Written by assemble.py alone (S08).

nodes.label: Workforce Extension Config (installed workforce-config.yml)
nodes.rationale: One config governs all three commands (S10, replaces categorize-config.yml + agents-config.yml); transcribed from specs/003-workforce/contracts/commands.md. D18 model:sonnet is the floor the runtime_consumed guard enforces in code.

nodes.label: general_cap (0.20 floor'd evidence cap)
nodes.rationale: FR-004 evidence floor (taxonomy §4): count(general) <= max(1, floor(0.20*count(tasks))) — v1 floor'd cap D65 verdict 9; validate-categorization.py enforces as exact integer ratio 1/5, over-cap ⇒ no artifact, phase fails (SC-002/S22).

nodes.label: assembly_cap (3-skill injection cap, D40)
nodes.rationale: assemble.py injects <=3 skills onto a base (ASSEMBLY_CAP); force-injected refactor-discipline counts against it; candidates trimmed beyond the cap are logged, never silently discarded (FR-011/SC-004).

nodes.label: Seed library manifest (7 bases + 5 skills)
nodes.rationale: The manifest install.sh reads to seed .claude/agents/ + .claude/skills/ additively (D17 per-repo library, outside any install rm -rf, S07). Bases curated-static (D44); skills alone evolve (D24). agt_backend_service/agt_data_persistence flagged provisional:true (S11).

nodes.label: skill_builder.web_search grant (D60 first elevated grant)
nodes.rationale: The system's FIRST elevated grant (owner ruling R1-S20): belongs to the skill-builder ROLE, not to modules it produces. Recorded on the dispatch trace as elevated_grants:[web_search] only when the builder actually searched (D43); a builder that didn't search stamps stale_risk:true (S17). Capability authorization ≠ dispatch approval (D63).

nodes.label: agt_generic (general-lane fallback base)
nodes.rationale: model:sonnet base for specialization:general — the FR-016 fallback lane, never a silent default; assemble.py exits 3 if it is missing from the library.

nodes.label: skl_refactor_discipline (auto-injected seed skill)
nodes.rationale: grants:[] seed skill auto-injected by assemble.py whenever preserves_behavior:true (taxonomy §2.3), counting against the assembly_cap of 3; the pattern the skill-builder models generated module bodies on.
